rules: Max 8 words. Sentence case. AU English. No em-dashes / en-dashes / smart quotes. No banned CSF phrases. Phase-tagged: waitlist headlines are pre-offer compliant (no transaction verbs, no $ minimum, no priority/allocation/queue); eoi headlines are interest-only non-binding (status draft-phase-gated); offer-live headlines are RG 234-balanced and anchor to 'the offer is live on Birchal' (status draft-phase-gated, the waitlist-scoped validator flags transaction verbs by design). Subject discipline: 'Birchal' = the company; the platform is 'our platform'. hook_audit.py standalone score >= 4 on EVERY headline against clients/birchal/facts.json. Unsourced market-leadership claims (64% / #1 / largest / first / $1B / $5M / 82,659 / 70% / '8 of the top ten') are NOT used; reframed to by-volume fact-backed equivalents only (324 raises, $234M+, 137,000, since 2018). See clients/birchal/loop/V5-client-email-FLAGS.md for the full client-email -> rule -> rewrite mapping.
v5_notes: Iterates BIRCHAL-HEADLINES-V4. New angles the client + JB asked for, all compliance-reframed: (a) 'Birchal Next' / vision -> forward-looking only ('what comes next', 'the next chapter'), never present-state capability; (b) raise-specific 'this is a live CSF offer' -> 'the next one is ours' / 'our offer is live' (phase-appropriate); (c) democratisation -> Aubrey Blanche category framing reframed to by-volume proof; (d) 'first in Australia / 64% / market leadership' -> NOT stated (no fact_id); reframed to by-volume ('324 raises', '$234M+', 'since 2018'). Every literal market-leadership claim the client used is logged in V5-client-email-FLAGS.md.

Every headline scored standalone with scripts/hook_audit.py --client-facts clients/birchal/facts.json. Minimum 4/5. Sub-4 candidates ('The next CSF offer is our own' 3, 'Investing that used to be VC-only' 2) were rewritten to by-volume cadence forms that score 4-5 (and demoted to ad descriptions where the angle was still wanted).

Phase-gated EOI/Offer-Live headlines are drafts: they ship only when that phase regime is active and must be re-cleared against live offer conditions (EOI) or the live disclosure document under RG 234 (Offer-Live).
halbert_targets: Every headline scores >= 4 on hook_audit.py standalone against clients/birchal/facts.json. At least 2 A-pile signals each: specificity (number/name/year), curiosity gap (why/what/how), pattern interrupt, or personal address. No statements without a hook.

headlines.text: 324 raises. The next one is ours.
Eight years funding others. Now our own.
You built this. Now we are raising.

headlines.caples_pattern: Use facts and figures + curiosity gap

headlines.text: What only VCs used to access
Why Dom Pym rates Birchal from both seats
headlines.note: Endorsement language only: 'rates' = Pym's verbatim endorsement (QUOTE-001), NOT an investment/cap-table claim. Inherits the V4-B2 VERIFY-PASS correction.

headlines.text: A real CSF raise. Tell us you're interested.
headlines.compliance_note: EOI-phase only; non-binding interest framing; re-clear against live conditions. 8 words (was 9: 'you are' contracted to 'you're' in the V5 adversarial verify pass to meet the 8-word rule; meaning and non-binding intent unchanged; Halbert standalone now 5).

headlines.text: 137,000 of you built this. Are you in?
headlines.compliance_note: EOI-phase only; 'Are you in?' is an interest signal, not a commitment; re-clear against live conditions.

headlines.text: Tell us if Birchal Next interests you
headlines.compliance_note: EOI-phase only; Birchal Next is forward-looking positioning, not a present-state claim; re-clear against live conditions.

headlines.caples_pattern: Use facts and figures + offer-live news
headlines.compliance_note: Offer-live phase only; pair with the risk-reversal body; requires RG 234 balance-test re-review against the live disclosure document; waitlist-scoped validator flags transaction verbs by design.

headlines.text: You built this. The offer is live.
headlines.compliance_note: Offer-live phase only; pair with the risk-reversal body; requires RG 234 balance-test re-review against the live disclosure document.

headlines.text: Why Dom Pym rates Birchal from both seats
headlines.compliance_note: Offer-live phase only; endorsement language only ('rates' = QUOTE-001 verbatim endorsement, not an investment claim); pair with the risk-reversal body; requires RG 234 balance-test re-review.

_compliance_summary: All 16 headlines scored standalone with scripts/hook_audit.py --client-facts clients/birchal/facts.json: every one >= 4 (min 4, several 5). Scanned against BANNED-CSF-PHRASES regex (zero matches), PREOFFER-VERBS regex for the waitlist set (zero transaction verbs + now/today/here; 'invested' in H5-W-CUR-01 and 'built'/'funding' are past-tense/gerund factual references to historical raises, not a current call to action), em-dash / en-dash / smart-quote check (zero), max-8-words rule (all <= 8; H5-E-RAISE-01 corrected from 9 to 8 in the V5 adversarial verify pass by contracting 'you are' to 'you're', meaning unchanged, Halbert standalone improved 4 -> 5). No literal '64% / #1 / largest / leading / first / 82,659 / $5M / $1B / 70% / 8-of-top-ten' claims: all reframed to by-volume fact-backed equivalents (324 raises, $234M+, 137,000, since 2018). EOI/Offer-Live headlines are status draft-phase-gated and carry per-headline compliance_note: they ship only when that phase is active and must be re-cleared (EOI: against live conditions; Offer-Live: under RG 234 against the live disclosure document; the waitlist-scoped validator flags 'invest'/transaction verbs by design for offer-live drafts).
